title: Flux Architecture
description: Essential commands and patterns for working with Flux architecture.

commands.description: Install the Flux library via npm
commands.scenario: Use when setting up a new project with Flux architecture.

commands.description: Import the Dispatcher from the Flux library
commands.scenario: Use when you need to create a new dispatcher in your Flux application.
Use to initialize a dispatcher for managing actions in Flux.

commands.description: Register a callback with the dispatcher
commands.scenario: Use when you need to listen for actions dispatched in the system.

commands.description: Dispatch an action to the dispatcher
commands.scenario: Use to send an action through the dispatcher to update stores.

commands.command: class Store extends EventEmitter { constructor() { super(); } }
commands.description: Create a basic store extending EventEmitter
commands.scenario: Use when defining a new store to handle application state.

commands.description: Emit a change event from the store
commands.scenario: Use to notify views that the store's data has changed.

commands.description: Listen for change events from the store
commands.scenario: Use in views to update UI when store data changes.

commands.command: const handleAction = (action) => { switch(action.type) { case 'ACTION_TYPE': // handle action break; } }
commands.scenario: Use to define how the store should respond to different actions.

commands.description: Wait for other stores to handle actions
commands.scenario: Use when a store's action handling depends on another store.

commands.command: class MyStore extends Store { constructor() { super(); this.dispatchToken = dispatcher.register(this.handleAction.bind(this)); } }
commands.description: Register a store with the dispatcher
commands.scenario: Use to connect a store to the dispatcher for action handling.
Use when creating a store that needs to emit events.
